candidates.title: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
candidates.summary: OpenAI released a set of six plug-ins aimed at specific jobs: data analytics, creative production, sales, product design, equity investing, and investment banking. Available from within the Codex app, each of the new tools bundles integrations, instructions, and context to allow Codex to approximate a specific job.

candidates.title: Improve your agent’s tool-calling accuracy with SFT and DPO on Amazon SageMaker AI
candidates.summary: In this post, you learn how to use Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) together to improve the tool-calling accuracy of a small language model (SLM). The example uses Amazon SageMaker AI training jobs, so you can focus on training code instead of managing your own training infrastructure. You also learn how to evaluate tool-calling accuracy and compare a base model to several fine-tuned variants, so you can make data-driven decisions about model quality.

candidates.title: Coralogix raises $200M on bet that someone needs to watch the AI agents
candidates.summary: Coralogix is among a growing number of infrastructure firms betting that as AI systems move into production, demand will rise for tools that can monitor their behavior, troubleshoot failures, and provide the operational data needed to keep them running reliably.

candidates.title: Introducing new capabilities to GPT-Rosalind
candidates.summary: GPT-Rosalind advances life sciences research with enhanced biological reasoning, medicinal chemistry expertise, genomics analysis, and experimental workflow capabilities.

candidates.title: A blueprint for democratic governance of frontier AI
candidates.summary: OpenAI outlines a blueprint for U.S. governance of frontier AI, proposing a federal framework for safety, resilience, and national security.

candidates.title: 5 ways Google Search can level up your thrift and vintage shopping
candidates.summary: Uncover second-hand scores with AI tools in Google Search and Shopping.

candidates.title: The art and science of hyperparameter optimization on Amazon Nova Forge
candidates.summary: Fine-tuning for domain-specific tasks means improving performance in one area without degrading the model’s general capabilities, and getting that balance right is harder than it looks. This post walks through how to navigate that balance, from selecting the right customization strategy for your data and task, to configuring the training parameters that most influence outcomes, like learning rate, batch size, and checkpointing. We also cover the common mistakes that lead to wasted training runs and how to catch them early, so you can improve domain performance without degrading general capabilities or burning through compute on avoidable failures. By the end, you will know how to improve domain performance without degrading general capabilities and how to avoid the expensive failures that come from getting the balance wrong.
